name: Battleship Missouri Memorial
tagline: Stand on the exact deck where WWII ended — the Mighty Mo pairs perfectly with the Arizona Memorial across the harbor.
parking: Use the Pearl Harbor Visitor Center lot ($7/day). Shuttle bus takes you to Ford Island
hours: Daily 8am–4pm. Closed Thanksgiving, Christmas, New Year's Day
bestTime: Morning — do the Arizona Memorial first (free, timed tickets), then shuttle to the Missouri. Plan 1.5–2 hours aboard
kamaaina: Kamaaina/military rates available with valid ID
talkStory: The USS Missouri is where General Douglas MacArthur accepted Japan's formal surrender on September 2, 1945, ending WWII. You can stand on the exact spot on the surrender deck — marked with a bronze plaque. The ship is massive (887 feet long) and you can explore multiple decks, crew quarters, the bridge, and massive 16-inch gun turrets. Look for the dent in the hull from a kamikaze strike. Combined with the Arizona Memorial, it bookends the Pacific War — the Arizona marks the beginning, the Missouri the end. Budget $35/adult for general admission; guided tours cost more but are worth it.
